title: ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026: Global Opportunity for Programme Development and International Development Professionals
summary: The International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property (ICCROM) has officially opened applications for its Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026, providing an exceptional opportunity for qualified professionals worldwide to contribute to international programmes, project development, donor engagement, partnerships, and cultural heritage initiatives. This global call seeks experienced consultants who can support ICCROM’s expanding Programme of Work through flexible, on-demand assignments. Successful candidates will be placed on a roster that remains valid for two years and may be called upon based on organizational priorities, funding availability, technical requirements, and consultant availability. For professionals with experience in programme development, project management, donor relations, fundraising, monitoring and evaluation, and international cooperation, this roster presents a unique opportunity to collaborate with one of the world s leading organizations dedicated to the preservation and restoration of cultural heritage. Application Deadline: 17 June 2026 (11:59 PM CEST) About ICCROM The International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property (ICCROM) is an intergovernmental organization headquartered in Rome, Italy. It works globally to promote the conservation and protection of cultural heritage through research, training, advisory services, partnerships, and capacity-building initiatives. As ICCROM continues to expand its strategic programmes and partnerships, the organization is creating a roster of Programme Support Specialists who can provide technical and operational support across various projects and initiatives. Overview of the Programme Support Specialist Roster The Programme Support Specialist roster is designed to provide ICCROM with rapid access to highly qualified consultants who can support programme development and implementation activities whenever needed. Key details include: Position: Programme Support Specialist Contract Type: Individual Engagement Contract Location: Rome, Italy, with possible travel as required Roster Validity: Two years, renewable based on organizational needs Compensation: Daily rate determined according to qualifications, experience, and assignment requirements Application Deadline: 17 June 2026 (11:59 PM CEST) Eligibility: Global It is important to note that inclusion on the roster does not constitute employment and does not guarantee assignment. Consultants may be engaged when specific organizational needs arise. Key Responsibilities of Programme Support Specialists Selected consultants may support a wide range of programme and project activities under the supervision of ICCROM staff members and in collaboration with relevant organizational units. Responsibilities may include: 1. Programme and Project Development Consultants may: Support the identification of new programme opportunities. Contribute to project design and development. Assist in creating funding proposals aligned with ICCROM’s Strategic Plan 2026–2031. Help ensure projects align with donor priorities and organizational goals. 2. Programme Implementation Support Responsibilities may include: Supporting ongoing programme delivery. Assisting with project implementation activities. Coordinating project-related processes and communications. Helping ensure programme objectives are achieved efficiently. 3. Donor Mapping and Funding Opportunity Identification Consultants may be expected to: Conduct donor research and mapping exercises. Identify potential funding opportunities. Analyze funding trends and partnership prospects. Maintain funding pipelines for future programme development. 4. Proposal Development and Grant Support Selected experts may assist in preparing: Concept notes Project proposals Logical frameworks Budgets Presentations Donor submissions This role is particularly attractive for professionals with strong proposal-writing and grants-management experience. 5. Stakeholder and Partnership Engagement Programme Support Specialists may: Facilitate consultations with stakeholders. Support coordination meetings. Engage with governments and public institutions. Collaborate with foundations and civil society organizations. Strengthen strategic partnerships. 6. Monitoring, Evaluation and Reporting Consultants may support: Monitoring and evaluation activities. Data collection and analysis. Lessons-learned exercises. Knowledge management initiatives. Donor reporting processes. 7. Communication and Visibility Activities Additional responsibilities may include: Drafting briefing notes. Preparing talking points. Supporting communication materials. Contributing to programme visibility initiatives. 8. Events, Missions and Coordination Depending on assignment requirements, consultants may: Support field missions. Participate in partnership initiatives. Assist in organizing meetings and events. Coordinate project-related activities. Required Educational Qualifications Applicants should possess: Essential Qualification An advanced university degree (Master’s degree or equivalent) in: International Development Political Science International Relations Economics Cultural Diplomacy Related disciplines Preferred Qualification Additional academic background in: Heritage Conservation Heritage Studies Related cultural heritage fields will be considered an advantage. Required Professional Experience ICCROM welcomes applications from both junior-level and mid-level professionals. Junior-Level Consultants Applicants should have: Between one and three years of progressively responsible professional experience. Mid-Level Consultants Applicants should have: Between three and seven years of progressively responsible professional experience. Relevant experience may include: Programme development Project implementation support Donor relations Fundraising Strategic planning Partnerships Monitoring and evaluation Grant management Proposal writing Skills and Competencies Successful candidates should demonstrate: Strong Writing and Communication Skills Applicants must be able to: Draft reports effectively. Prepare project proposals. Write briefing materials. Produce concise and professional documentation. Research and Analytical Capacity Candidates should be able to: Conduct background research. Analyze complex information. Synthesize findings from multiple sources. Project Management Knowledge Understanding of: Project cycle management Results-based management principles Monitoring and evaluation frameworks is highly valuable. Interpersonal and Teamwork Skills ICCROM seeks professionals who can: Work effectively in multicultural environments. Build positive stakeholder relationships. Collaborate across diverse teams. Organizational Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Ability to manage multiple assignments. Capacity to work under tight deadlines. Strong planning and coordination skills. Technical Skills Applicants should possess proficiency in: Microsoft Word Microsoft Excel Microsoft PowerPoint Knowledge of the cultural heritage sector will be an added advantage. Language Requirements Mandatory Fluency in English (written and spoken) Highly Desirable Knowledge of: French Any other official United Nations language will strengthen an application. Selection Process ICCROM will evaluate applicants based on the qualifications and competencies outlined in the call. The selection process may include: Application screening Skills assessments or tests Competency-based interviews Final roster placement Only candidates who meet the required qualifications may be included in the roster. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents in PDF format: 1. Motivation Letter A well-written motivation letter explaining: Relevant experience Professional achievements Interest in the role Suitability for the roster 2. Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) The CV must: Be written in English Reflect current qualifications and experience Highlight relevant programme and project work Applications missing any required documents will not be considered. How to Apply Interested applicants should send their applications in English to: Email: rosters@iccrom.org Important Application Instructions Use Programme Support Specialist as the email subject line. Submit all required documents in PDF format. Ensure all materials are complete before submission. No modifications can be made after submission. Important Notes for Applicants Before applying, candidates should note the following: Inclusion on the roster does not guarantee employment. Consultants will be contacted based on organizational requirements. Funding availability may influence engagement opportunities. Technical suitability and consultant availability will be considered for assignments. All applications will be treated confidentially. ICCROM s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion ICCROM is committed to maintaining the highest standards of efficiency, competence, and integrity. The organization actively promotes: Gender equality Workforce diversity Geographic representation Cultural inclusion Equal opportunities ICCROM also maintains a zero-tolerance policy against all forms of harassment and strives to build a diverse and inclusive professional environment. For qualified professionals seeking international consulting opportunities in programme management, donor relations, project development, fundraising, and cultural heritage, the ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026 offers a valuable pathway to contribute to impactful global initiatives while working alongside a respected international organization. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . The post ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026: Global Opportunity for Programme Development and International Development Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Join the Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education 2026–2027
summary: Help Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence in Education Across England The Children’s Commissioner for England is inviting passionate and forward-thinking young people to become members of a newly established Youth Advisory Board focused on Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Education. This exciting opportunity gives young people a direct platform to share their experiences, perspectives, and ideas about how AI is influencing learning, teaching, and education across England. As AI technologies continue to transform classrooms, learning tools, and educational experiences, it is increasingly important that young people have a voice in the conversations that shape future policies. The Youth Advisory Board has been created to ensure that policymakers hear directly from the young people who are most affected by these changes. Through regular meetings, discussions, and collaborative engagement with policymakers, board members will play a meaningful role in helping the Department for Education understand the realities of young people s experiences with AI and contribute to recommendations that could influence educational policy for years to come. About the Youth Advisory Board The Youth Advisory Board is a group of young people brought together to provide valuable insights into the opportunities and challenges associated with Artificial Intelligence in education. Members of the board will have the opportunity to: Share their personal experiences of using AI in educational settings. Discuss how AI is affecting learning, teaching, assessment, and student wellbeing. Highlight concerns, opportunities, and recommendations regarding AI in schools and colleges. Contribute to discussions that may influence future educational policies. Ensure that the voices of young people remain at the center of decisions about educational technology. The initiative recognizes that young people are among the most active users of emerging technologies and therefore have unique insights that can help shape effective and responsible AI policies. Benefits of Joining the Youth Advisory Board Successful participants will enjoy several valuable benefits, including: 1. Influence National Education Policy Members will have a direct opportunity to inform discussions and policy recommendations related to Artificial Intelligence in Education. Their experiences and opinions will help policymakers better understand how AI is impacting young people. 2. Develop Leadership Skills Participation in the board will help members strengthen essential skills such as: Public speaking Communication Critical thinking Policy engagement Teamwork Problem-solving Advocacy 3. Collaborate with Other Young Leaders Participants will join a diverse group of young people from across England who are passionate about education, technology, and creating positive change. 4. Gain Exposure to Emerging Technologies Board members will deepen their understanding of Artificial Intelligence and its growing role within education systems. 5. Make a Lasting Impact The recommendations and insights generated by the board may contribute to future decisions that affect children and young people throughout England. Meeting Structure and Commitment Successful applicants should be prepared to actively participate throughout the duration of the program. Key Commitments Include: Introductory In-Person Meeting Attend an introductory face-to-face meeting in London during July 2026 (exact date to be confirmed). Regular Advisory Board Meetings Meet every six weeks with fellow advisory board members. Participate in meetings until December 2027. Hybrid Participation Format The advisory board will use a combination of in-person and online engagement: Four meetings will be held in person. Remaining meetings will be conducted online. This structure ensures meaningful collaboration while allowing flexibility for participants from different parts of England. Why Artificial Intelligence in Education Matters Artificial Intelligence is rapidly becoming part of everyday educational experiences. Students increasingly encounter AI-powered learning platforms, educational assistants, assessment tools, and digital learning resources. The Children’s Commissioner for England recognizes that young people are uniquely positioned to provide feedback on: How AI supports learning. Potential risks and challenges associated with AI use. Fairness and accessibility concerns. Privacy and safety considerations. The future role of technology in education. The Youth Advisory Board provides a valuable opportunity for young people to contribute to these important discussions and ensure that educational policies reflect real-world experiences. Eligibility Requirements To be eligible for the Youth Advisory Board, applicants must: Live in England. Be between 16 and 18 years old in July 2026. Be willing to participate in both online and in-person meetings. Have an interest in discussing Artificial Intelligence and its role in education. Be prepared to contribute their views and experiences throughout the advisory period. Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible once applications are available. APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education offers an exceptional opportunity for young people to influence national conversations about the future of education. By sharing their experiences, insights, and recommendations, participants can help ensure that Artificial Intelligence develops in ways that genuinely benefit children and young people across England. Young people who are passionate about technology, education, and creating positive change should consider applying and becoming part of this important initiative. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Join the Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education 2026–2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Emory University Careers: Explore Faculty, Staff, Temporary, and Internal Employment Opportunities at Emory University
summary: Emory University is one of the leading institutions committed to excellence in education, research, healthcare, and community engagement. Through its comprehensive careers platform, Emory University offers a wide range of employment opportunities for professionals seeking meaningful and impactful careers in higher education and related fields. Whether you are an experienced academic, an administrative professional, a researcher, a support staff member, or someone looking for temporary employment opportunities, Emory University provides multiple pathways for career growth and professional development. Why Consider a Career at Emory University? Working at Emory University means becoming part of a respected institution that values innovation, collaboration, diversity, and service. The university continually seeks talented individuals who can contribute to its mission while advancing their own professional goals. Career opportunities at Emory span various disciplines and departments, allowing candidates with diverse educational and professional backgrounds to find positions aligned with their expertise and aspirations. Available Career Opportunities at Emory University The university s employment portal provides access to several categories of job opportunities, including: 1. Faculty Positions Faculty opportunities are available for individuals interested in teaching, research, academic leadership, and scholarly contributions. These positions may include: Professors Associate Professors Assistant Professors Lecturers Research Faculty Academic Leadership Roles Faculty members play a critical role in advancing the university s educational and research mission while mentoring students and contributing to their respective fields. 2. Staff Jobs Staff positions support the university s operations and strategic objectives. These opportunities may be available across multiple departments and functional areas such as: Administration Human Resources Finance Information Technology Student Services Research Administration Facilities Management Communications and Marketing Healthcare Support Services Staff members contribute significantly to maintaining the institution s excellence and operational effectiveness. 3. Temporary Jobs Temporary employment opportunities provide flexible work arrangements for individuals seeking short-term assignments or project-based roles. Benefits of temporary positions may include: Gaining valuable work experience Building professional networks Developing new skills Exploring career pathways within the university environment Temporary positions can also serve as an excellent entry point for individuals interested in future opportunities within the institution. 4. Employee Internal Job Portal Current employees can access the Employee Internal Job Portal to explore additional career advancement opportunities within Emory University. Internal mobility allows employees to: Pursue professional growth Expand their skill sets Transition into new departments Advance their careers while remaining within the university community Commitment to Equal Opportunity Employment Emory University is committed to maintaining an inclusive and equitable workplace. The institution states that it is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to: Race Color Religion Sex National origin Disability Protected veteran status Other characteristics protected by applicable state or federal laws This commitment reflects Emory University s dedication to fostering a workplace where individuals from diverse backgrounds can thrive and contribute meaningfully. Resources Available to Applicants The university provides access to several resources designed to support prospective and current employees, including: Student Resources Alumni Resources Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) Career Assistance and Support Services Employment Information and Guidance These resources help applicants navigate the recruitment process and better understand available career opportunities. How to Explore Opportunities Interested candidates can browse available openings through the Emory University Careers portal and search opportunities within the following categories: Faculty Positions Staff Jobs Temporary Jobs Internal Employee Opportunities Applicants are encouraged to review position requirements carefully and submit complete applications that demonstrate their qualifications, experience, and suitability for the role. APPLY HERE Build Your Career at Emory University For professionals seeking an opportunity to work within a highly respected academic institution, Emory University offers diverse career pathways across teaching, research, administration, and support services. With its commitment to equal opportunity employment, professional development, and institutional excellence, Emory University continues to attract talented individuals who are passionate about making a positive impact through their work. There is currently no application deadline specified in the provided information. Interested applicants should apply as early as possible and regularly monitor the careers portal for new openings and updates. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here .
